---
title: "Stiff Marsh Bedstraw (Galium tinctorium)"
slug: "galium-tinctorium"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621846691/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Stiff Marsh Bedstraw"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Gentianales"
tax_family: "Rubiaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Gentianales", "Rubiaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/621846691/medium.jpg" alt="Stiff Marsh Bedstraw" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jimw3">
jimw3</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341573752">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Gentianales
-   **Family:** Rubiaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=136375)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, wet flatwoods, and along pond margins.

## Natural history notes

A slender, sprawling plant characterized by its narrow leaves arranged in whorls along the stem and small white to greenish flowers. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, wet flatwoods, and along pond margins. It grows during the warmer months and often forms low mats among other wetland vegetation.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](http://inaturalist.org/taxa/136375-Galium-tinctorium)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3643)
